brief_document.title: Unify activity dispatch onto the live round-robin path
brief_document.purpose: Two activity-dispatch implementations coexist in crates/aion-server/src/worker, and only the weaker one is wired to production. The live engine seam is WorkerActivityDispatcher in bridge.rs (constructed in state.rs): it selects a single worker via registry.select_worker (lowest id, no rotation) and, in send_activity_task, fails the whole dispatch the moment that one worker's channel is full or closed — there is no try-next. The richer ActivityDispatcher in dispatch.rs (rotation via workers_for, deregister-and-try-next on a closed channel, wait-for-worker) is constructed nowhere in production — only inside its own unit tests. The consequence is that AW-014's round-robin, even once landed, does not actually distribute load in production: the live path picks one worker, and AW-014's R2/R3 add rotation and the closed-channel fallback to the UNUSED dispatch.rs path. This brief makes round-robin and the closed-channel fallback real on the live bridge.rs path and retires the duplicate dispatcher so a single dispatch path remains. It depends on AW-014's R1 (the per-(namespace, activity_type) rotation index on the registry — the path-agnostic primitive) and supersedes AW-014's R2/R3, which target the dead path. Recommendation, for Tom to decide: trim AW-014 to R1 (+R4) so no rotation/fallback code is written into dispatch.rs only to be deleted here.
brief_document.task: Change the live dispatcher (crates/aion-server/src/worker/bridge.rs) so that selecting and delivering an activity task walks the registry's rotation-ordered candidate list for the activity's (namespace, activity_type) key instead of picking a single lowest-id worker: attempt delivery in rotation order, deregister any worker whose channel is closed, advance to the next, and only fail with the existing no-worker / all-closed errors once every candidate is exhausted — preserving the existing await-result model (insert the pending completion, await the result on its receiver) and the drain check before each attempt. Then retire the duplicate dispatcher in crates/aion-server/src/worker/dispatch.rs: delete the unused ActivityDispatcher push struct and ScheduledActivity (constructed only in their own tests), keeping the completion machinery (ActivityCompletion, ActivityCompletionOutcome, ActivityCompletionSink, handle_activity_result, lost_worker_error) that the live path and worker_grpc.rs use, and update the worker/mod.rs re-exports. Move the closed-worker-skip and round-robin distribution test scenarios onto the live WorkerActivityDispatcher. No new event type, no wire protocol change, no change to the activity completion contract or the await-result dispatch model.

requirements.title: Live dispatch selects in rotation order with closed-channel fallback
requirements.spec: WHEN WorkerActivityDispatcher dispatches a scheduled activity, THE SYSTEM SHALL obtain the registry's rotation-ordered candidate list for the activity's (namespace, activity_type) key (the AW-014 rotation primitive) and attempt delivery to each candidate in that order. IF a candidate's channel is full or closed, THE SYSTEM SHALL deregister that worker and advance to the next candidate in rotation order, without skipping any remaining candidate. The first candidate whose channel accepts the task wins, and the existing await-result model is preserved: the pending completion is registered for the chosen worker and the dispatching call awaits the result on its receiver exactly as today. IF every candidate's channel is closed, THE SYSTEM SHALL return the existing worker_dispatch error 'all matching worker streams closed before task could be delivered'. IF no worker is registered for the key, THE SYSTEM SHALL retain the existing wait-for-worker behaviour and the existing no-worker error path. The drain_state.ensure_accepting check SHALL be evaluated before each delivery attempt in the fallback loop.
acceptance: Dispatching 3 activities of the same type through WorkerActivityDispatcher with 3 registered live workers results in each worker's receiver getting exactly one task — successive dispatches begin at different workers (round-robin reaches the live path, not just the registry helper in isolation).
Dispatching 6 activities across 3 live workers distributes approximately evenly — a test asserts each worker received 2.

A dispatch where the rotated-to worker's channel is closed deregisters that worker and delivers to the next candidate in rotation order; a test asserts the registry count drops and the surviving worker's receiver has the task.

A dispatch where every candidate's channel is closed returns Err(ServerError::WorkerDispatch { .. }) with the existing message text, and the pending completion registered for the activity is cleaned up (no leaked pending entry, no leaked heartbeat tracking).

The drain check still runs before each attempt in the fallback loop — a test that flips the drain state to draining mid-iteration confirms the dispatch fails with the drain error, not a channel error.
send_activity_task no longer fails the whole dispatch on the first closed channel: the single-pick-and-fail behaviour is gone, replaced by the rotation-ordered fallback loop.

requirements.title: Retire the duplicate dispatcher; one dispatch path remains
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL remove the unused ActivityDispatcher push struct and the ScheduledActivity value from crates/aion-server/src/worker/dispatch.rs and their unit tests, because they are constructed nowhere in production — only inside their own tests — and the live WorkerActivityDispatcher (R1) is now the sole dispatch path. THE SYSTEM SHALL keep the completion machinery that the live path and the gRPC completion handler depend on: ActivityCompletion, ActivityCompletionOutcome, ActivityCompletionSink, handle_activity_result, and lost_worker_error. THE SYSTEM SHALL update the crates/aion-server/src/worker/mod.rs re-export list to drop ActivityDispatcher and ScheduledActivity while retaining the completion exports. No #[deprecated] marker, no compatibility shim, no zombie code — the dead types are deleted outright (NO BACKWARDS COMPATIBILITY).
acceptance: grep for 'ActivityDispatcher' and 'ScheduledActivity' across crates/aion-server returns no definition and no production or test construction site after the change (the names are gone, not merely unused).
worker/mod.rs no longer re-exports ActivityDispatcher or ScheduledActivity, and still re-exports ActivityCompletion, ActivityCompletionOutcome, ActivityCompletionSink, and handle_activity_result.
bridge.rs and api/worker_grpc.rs continue to import and use the retained completion types unchanged; the completion path (handle_activity_result / ActivityCompletionSink) is untouched.
The crate builds with no dead_code warnings and no unused-import warnings introduced by the deletion.

requirements.title: Invariants hold across the diff
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL introduce no new Event variant, no new wire protocol message, and no change to the worker registration handshake, the activity completion contract, or the await-result dispatch model. No code added by this brief SHALL call EventStore::append directly. The rotation index SHALL remain in-memory only (AW-014's primitive) — not persisted, serialised, or exposed on the wire. The added and modified code SHALL be free of #[allow], #[expect], #[ignore], unwrap, and expect in non-test library code; lock and channel poison SHALL be handled explicitly and mapped to a typed error. WHEN the brief lands, cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, and the full workspace test suite SHALL pass clean.
acceptance: git diff introduces no new variant in any proto or wire type; grep for 'rotation' in aion-proto returns no matches.
grep -nE '#\[allow|#\[expect|#\[ignore|\.unwrap\(\)|\.expect\(' over the non-test lines of the modified files in crates/aion-server/src/worker/ returns no matches introduced by this brief.

cargo fmt --check exits 0; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings exits 0; cargo test -p aion-server passes, including the relocated round-robin and closed-channel tests on the live dispatcher.
No file in crates/aion-server/src/worker/ exceeds the 500-line code limit after the change (deleting the dead dispatcher reduces dispatch.rs; bridge.rs stays within budget).

boundaries: SHALL NOT change the await-result dispatch model (register pending completion, await the result on its receiver) — this brief unifies selection and delivery, not the completion model.

SHALL NOT remove the shared completion machinery in dispatch.rs (ActivityCompletion, ActivityCompletionOutcome, ActivityCompletionSink, handle_activity_result, lost_worker_error) — only the unused ActivityDispatcher push struct and ScheduledActivity are deleted.
SHALL NOT persist, serialise, or expose the rotation index — it is AW-014's volatile in-memory primitive.
SHALL NOT add a load-aware dispatch strategy (channel depth, latency, CPU) or any configuration knob for the dispatch strategy — round-robin remains the only strategy.
SHALL NOT change the wire protocol, add a new proto message, modify the worker registration handshake, or introduce a new Event variant.
SHALL NOT re-introduce AW-014's R2/R3 edits to dispatch.rs — the live path in bridge.rs is the single dispatcher this brief leaves behind.
verification: cargo test -p aion-server passes, including the round-robin distribution and closed-channel fallback tests now asserted against WorkerActivityDispatcher.

Register 3 live workers for one activity type, dispatch 3 activities through WorkerActivityDispatcher, and observe each worker's channel received exactly 1 task — round-robin reaches production (R1, S14).
Register 3 live workers, close the rotated-to worker's receiver, dispatch 1 activity, and observe the next worker in rotation order receives it and the closed worker is deregistered (R1).
grep confirms ActivityDispatcher and ScheduledActivity are gone from aion-server and the completion machinery remains (R2).
No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new or modified code.

checklist.text: The dispatch method starts iteration from the rotation index, wrapping around the sorted worker list; a closed-channel send deregisters the worker and tries the next in rotation order; the existing error for no-workers and all-closed-channels is preserved.

stories.text: As an operator scaling workers horizontally, I want dispatched activities round-robin'd across connected workers so that adding workers actually distributes load instead of every activity going to the lowest-ID worker.

constraints.text: No #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] lint bypasses per CLAUDE.md; tests needing a running server gate at runtime (a *_TEST_URL env var with a logged skip), never #[ignore].
lib.rs/mod.rs are declarations and re-exports only.
aion-proto is a leaf crate: it depends on aion-core and external crates only — no aion, no aion-server, no store backend. Structural; must hold (per D2).
aion-server depends on aion, aion-proto, and a store backend among workspace crates; it contains no workflow execution logic — it delegates every durable decision to the Engine (per D5).
Wire data types (proto messages, streamed events, summaries) derive Serialize + Deserialize; config types that carry secrets are Deserialize-only (D4, project credential policy).
Library errors use thiserror (WireError in proto, ServerError in server); anyhow appears only in aion-server's top-level main. No unwrap/expect in library code; lock/stream poison handled explicitly.
No cross-namespace operation reaches the engine: namespace authorisation and scoping happen at the adapter boundary before any Engine call (per D8).
A slow or stalled WebSocket consumer is dropped with a typed lag error; per-connection buffers are bounded; the engine broadcast is never back-pressured by a subscriber (per D6).
No hardcoded operational defaults (ports, heartbeat window, buffer bound, timeouts): all from config; engine-owned defaults deferred to the engine (per CLAUDE.md 'no assumed defaults').
The wire Event/WorkflowFilter/WorkflowSummary/WorkflowStatus and ID types are aion-core's types, not re-declared wire clones (per D2).

resolved_context.intention: Build the network skin of Aion. The aion engine crate is transport-agnostic by design — no HTTP, gRPC, or WebSocket — so a CLI embeds it with nothing but a file-backed store. Teams running many services want a managed deployment: one binary clients and workers point at, that streams live workflow events to a dashboard, isolates tenants, and dispatches activity tasks to out-of-process workers in any language. That is aion-server, and the wire contract it speaks is aion-proto. aion-proto is a pure leaf-level contract — gRPC service definitions plus serde wire types — that the server, every client SDK (AL), and every worker SDK (AR) agree on, defined once so they never drift. aion-server is the deployable: it wraps a live Engine, translates wire requests into Engine calls, tails the engine's event broadcast to feed WebSocket subscribers, runs the server side of the remote-worker protocol, partitions everything by namespace, and serves the dashboard's API and static assets. It adds no execution logic — every durable decision still happens in aion.
